cb:div.cb:mulu: Preface to the Recarved Record of Patriarch Zhaozhou
cb:div.head: Machine translation using ChatGPT 5.2Preface to the Recarved Record of Patriarch Zhaozhou
cb:div.p:
I have heard of one who could ruin households and scatter estates, and at eighteen was skilled
in wielding the Tai'e sword; who let guest and host seize one another, and before worthies and sages
made a play of every encounter: if not a single thing is brought forth, he simply tells one to put it down;
when not a single thought arises, he points and says it is Mount Sumeru.
Each time he picks up a single blade of grass and calls it the sixteen-foot golden body;
with but a single tooth in his mouth, he fully knows all the flavors of the world.
The turnip of Zhenzhou, people in all quarters idly say, is precisely his transmitted teaching;
A Qingzhou cloth robe; students, do not seek the target within words.
A plain and honest old fellow, killing and giving life according to the moment, suddenly surpassing those
who wield sticks and shouts; with a soft tongue of three inches, freely
at ease in all directions, when did he ever use strange words or bizarre speech? It is like
pressing down a gourd on water, lowering the hand and pressing east, pressing west; in the room hangs
a precious mirror, letting commoners come or sages come. When raising up the supreme
vehicle of the lineage, if one speaks of Buddha-recitation he would rinse his mouth for three days; well able to drag
mud and carry water, answering being and nonbeing. The true son of Nanquan,As asked, so it follows.
the grandson of Mazu, was only this old man alone
indeed. In his manifested traces, worms entered his mouth and he ate chaff
and was at peace; in the turning of karmic recompense, a bright pearl emerged from the sea and the two
kings made offerings. If so, how could he not be an ancient Buddha manifested
for the benefit of beings? It is a pity his recorded sayings could not all be transmitted,
and students obtained only one fascicle, truly like tasting one morsel from a cauldron,
or drinking a single drop from the sea. But the old printing, after many years, has characters
blurred and indistinct, so our disciple Mingsheng brought forth the resolve to recut and make it known
for later generations. This truly repays the Patriarch's kindness and deeply accords with this old
worn one's intention. Admiring his aspiration, I have casually appended a few words. As for
his complete functioning and great activity, my poor intelligence cannot explain it; reaching depths and
penetrating subtleties, there are naturally masters of broad understanding for that.

Yunmen, 27th generation transmitter of the orthodox Caodong lineage,
written by Sanmu Yuancheng, abbot of Xiansheng Monastery

cb:div.cb:mulu: Prince Zhao composed a portrait eulogy for the Master
cb:div.head: Prince Zhao composed a portrait eulogy for the Master

lg.l: Moon over the blue stream, a clear mirror at the center. My master, my transformation, Zhaozhou under heaven.

The Master left the waters and stirred the hearts of kings and lords; the seal-light sank, the fly-whisk was put away; in the blue sky mist veils the pine ridges
and moon, on the vast deep waves cover the ferrying boat. One lamp suddenly goes out and Mara rejoices; both eyes grow dim again and fellow practitioners
grieve. Though one be a lucid traveler beyond the clouds, whenever gazing at the bottle and seat, tears still flow.

The Buddha-sun tilts west, the Patriarch-seal jewel is lost; the cinnabar pool sinks, the moon sinks in radiance, shadows spread through the abbot's room with furnace smoke
in gloom; wind rises in the meditation hall, the pine tones are faint. A single sandal once came and left traces of transformation; in what place of the five Indias shall we again
meet his return? The disciple who understands emptiness cuts off sorrow and joy, yet still sheds tears before the snowy curtain.

cb:juan.cb:jhead: Recorded Sayings of Monk Zhaozhou, Upper Scroll

cb:div.byline: Recorded by the disciple Wenyuan, a student of the Way
Re-collated by Dacan, the Daoist Liren
Recut by Mingsheng, disciple of Yunmen
cb:div.p:
The Master asked Nanquan, What is the Way? Quan said, Ordinary mind is it. The Master said, Can it still be directed toward
or pursued? Quan said, If you intend it, you deviate. The Master said, If one does not intend, how does one know it is the Way? Quan said, The Way does not belong to knowing
or not knowing. Knowing is false awareness; not knowing is blankness. If one truly reaches the undoubting Way, it is like
great empty space, vast and open and unobstructed. How could one force right and wrong upon it? At these words the Master suddenly awakened to the mysterious meaning,
his mind like a bright moon.

Nanquan ascended the hall. The Master asked, Does the bright side accord, or does the dark side accord? Quan immediately returned to his room. The Master then left the
hall and said, This old master, once I asked him, was left with no words to answer. The head monk said, Do not
say the master had no words; it is only that you do not understand. The Master struck him and then said, This blow ought to be
for the old man in the abbot's seat to take.

The Master asked Nanquan, Where does one who knows there is such a thing go? Quan said, To the patron's house in front of the mountain
to become a water buffalo. The Master said, Thank you, master, for the instruction. Quan said, Last night at the third watch
the moon came to the window.

While the Master served as stove attendant at Nanquan's, the assembly was on a communal work shift picking vegetables. The Master shouted inside the hall, Fire!
Fire! The whole assembly rushed at once to the monks' hall, and the Master then shut the hall door. The assembly had no
reply. Quan then tossed the key through the window into the hall, and the Master opened the door.

The Master was drawing water at Nanquan's well tower. Seeing Nanquan pass by, he grabbed a post and hung up his feet,
saying, Save me! Save me! Nanquan climbed the ladder and said, One, two, three, four, five. After a little while the Master
came down, bowed in thanks, and said, I thank master for just now saving me.

Nanquan's east and west halls were disputing over a cat. Quan came into the hall, lifted up the cat, and said, If you can speak it,
I will not cut it; if you cannot speak it, I will cut it. The assembly's responses all failed to meet Quan's meaning, and at once
he cut the cat. By evening the Master returned from outside. During greetings, Quan then raised
the earlier matter and said, How would you have saved the cat? The Master then put one sandal on
his head and went out. Quan said, If you had been there, the cat would have been saved.

The Master asked Nanquan, Difference I will not ask about. What is sameness? Quan placed both hands on the ground, and the Master
kicked him over, then returned to the Nirvana Hall crying, Regret! Regret! Hearing this, Quan sent someone to ask, Regret what
exactly? The Master said, I regret not giving him two more kicks.

Nanquan passed by the bathhouse and saw the bath attendant tending the fire. He asked, What are you doing? The man said, Heating the bath.
Quan said, Remember to come call the water buffalo for a bath. The bath attendant replied, Yes. That evening, the attendant entered
the abbot's room. Quan asked, What are you doing? He said, Inviting the water buffalo to bathe. Quan said, Did you bring rope
and halter or not? The bath attendant had no reply. The Master came to greet Quan, and Quan told him the story. The Master said, I have
a word. Quan immediately said, Did you bring rope and halter? The Master stepped forward and suddenly grabbed Quan's nose and pulled. Quan
said, Yes, yes, but too rough.

The Master asked Nanquan, Beyond the four propositions and apart from the hundred negations, please speak, Master. Quan then returned to his room. The Master
said, This old master is usually so glib, but when actually questioned, he cannot set forth a single word. The attendant
said, Do not say the master has no words. The Master slapped him. Nanquan then shut the abbot's
room door, made a ring of ashes around it, and asked the monks, If you can speak it, I will open the door. Many gave responses
but none accorded with Quan's meaning. The Master said, Blue heaven! Blue heaven! Quan then opened the door.

The Master asked Nanquan, Mind is not Buddha, wisdom is not the Way. Is there still fault in this or not? Quan said,
There is. The Master said, Where is the fault? Please speak, Master. Quan was about to raise it, and the Master immediately left.

The Master ascended the hall and told the assembly: This matter is exact and direct; even great people of immeasurable stature cannot get out of it. This old monk
once went to Guishan, where a monk asked, What is the Patriarch's meaning in coming from the West? Guishan said, Bring me
the chair. If one is truly a lineage master, one must receive people with the fundamental matter. Then a monk asked:
What is the Patriarch's meaning in coming from the West? The Master said, The cypress tree in the courtyard. The student said, master, do not
use an object to instruct people. The Master said, I am not using an object to instruct people. He said, What is the Patriarch's meaning in coming from the West?
The Master said, The cypress tree in the courtyard. The Master also said, Ninety years ago this old monk saw under Master Mazu
more than eighty good teachers, every one of them a true adept, unlike the teachers of today
who grow branches upon branches, all greatly far removed from the sages. Each generation is worse than the
last. For example, Nanquan always said, One must proceed among those of a different kind. But how do you understand that? Now
yellow-mouthed youngsters stand at crossroads spinning vines of talk, angling for food and seeking bows, gathering
three or five hundred people, saying, I am a good teacher, you are students.

A monk asked, What is a pure monastery? The Master said, A girl with hair in two horns. What is the person in the monastery?
The Master said, A girl with hair in two horns who is pregnant.
A monk asked, I have heard that master personally saw Nanquan, is that so? The Master said, Zhenzhou produces big radishes.
A monk asked, Where is master's native place? The Master pointed with his hand and said, West, and farther west.
A monk asked, The dharma has no separate dharma; what is dharma? The Master said, Outer emptiness, inner emptiness, inner-and-outer emptiness.
A monk asked, What is the Buddha's true Dharma-body? The Master said, What more is there to dislike?
A monk asked, What is the mind-ground Dharma gate? The Master said, The pattern-board of past and present.

A monk asked, What is host within guest? The Master said, This mountain monk does not ask about wives. What is guest within host?
The Master said, This old monk has no father-in-law.

A monk asked, What is the abiding permanence of all dharmas? The Master said, This old monk does not avoid speaking of the ancestors. The monk asked again,
and the Master said, Today I will not answer.

The Master ascended the hall and said: Brothers, do not stand long. If there is business, discuss it; if there is none, go sit beneath your robe and bowl
and investigate thoroughly. When this old monk traveled, aside from the two meal times, which were places for mixed use of effort, all the rest
had no other place to apply mind. If it is not like this, leaving home is far indeed.

A monk asked, Among all things, what is the hardest? The Master said, In mutual cursing, I yield you catching mouths; in mutual spitting, I yield you
splashing water.
A monk asked, What about when it does not stop day or night? The Master said, Among monks there are no such twice-taxed commoners.

A monk asked, What is the one phrase? The Master said, If you cling to one phrase, it will age you. The Master also said, If
all your life you never leave the monastery grove and do not speak for ten years or five, no one will call you mute, and later
even the Buddha will be unable to do anything with you. If you do not believe it, cut off this old monk's head.

The Master ascended the hall and said: Brothers, you are right in the third grievance. Therefore it is said, Only change the place where your former
conduct was carried out; do not change the former person. You and I each left home on our own; lately with nothing to do, you furthermore
ask about Chan and ask about the Way, thirty or twenty people gathering their heads to ask, just as if I owed them Chan and the Way.
You call me a good teacher, but I am one receiving the cudgel together with you. This old monk does not joke for pleasure; I fear I may burden
the ancient ones, and so I speak east and speak west.

A monk asked, How should one use mind throughout the twelve hours? The Master said, You are used by the twelve hours; this old monk uses
the twelve hours. Which hour are you asking about?

A monk asked, What is the host of Zhaozhou? The Master barked, You barrel-hooping fellow. The student answered, Yes.
The Master said, Hoop the barrel according to the rule.
A monk asked, What is the student's fundamental matter? The Master said, The tree shakes and birds scatter; fish are startled and the water turns muddy.

A monk asked, What is a person of little spirit? The Master said, This old monk is not equal to you. The student said, I do not claim victory.
The Master said, Then why are you of little spirit?

A monk asked, The supreme Way is not difficult, only it dislikes choosing and picking. Is this a den for people of the time? The Master said, Once someone asked me this,
and for five years straight I could not sort it out.

An official asked, When Danxia burned the wooden Buddha, why did the monastery steward's eyebrows and beard fall out? The Master said:
In your household, who is it that turns the raw into cooked? The official said, The servant. The Master said, Then that is indeed his
special skill.

A monk asked, When the seer Vimukta held Sudhana's hand and he saw Buddhas in motes of dust, how was it? The Master then grasped the monk's
hand and said, What do you see?

A nun asked, What is the conduct of a śramaṇa? The Master said, Do not bear children. The nun said, master, do not engage
with me. The Master said, If I were to engage with you, what would I amount to?
A monk asked, What is the host of Zhaozhou? The Master said, A field-granary slave.
A monk asked, What is the king asking for xian-tuo-po? The Master said, Tell me, what do you say this old monk wants?
A monk asked, What is the mystery within mystery? The Master said, What mystery within mystery are you talking about? Seven within seven, eight within eight.
A monk asked, What is xian-tuo-po? The Master said, In quiet places, svaha.

A monk asked, What is dharma and non-dharma? The Master said, East, west, south, north. The student said, How should one understand it? The Master
said, Up, down, and the four corners.
A monk asked, What is the mystery within mystery? The Master said, If that monk were still here, he would be seventy-four or seventy-five.
A monk asked, When the king asks for xian-tuo-po, then what? The Master suddenly rose, bowed, and folded his hands.
A monk asked, What is the Way? The Master said, I dare not, I dare not.
A monk asked, What is dharma? The Master said, Chik chik, she she.

A monk asked, How far is Zhaozhou from Zhenfu? The Master said, Three hundred. The student said, How far from Zhenfu to Zhaozhou
is it? The Master said, Not separated.

A monk asked, What is the mystery within mystery? The Master said, How long has mystery been here? The student said, Mystery has been here a long time.
The Master said, Good thing you met this old monk, otherwise mystery would have killed this many-times-born one.
A monk asked, What is the student's self? The Master said, Do you still see the cypress tree in the courtyard?

The Master ascended the hall and said: If you are one who has long practiced, nothing is not real, nothing is not spanning ancient and present;
if you are newly entered among the assembly, you too must investigate principle before it will do. Do not chase after this side's three hundred, five
hundred, one thousand, with the two assemblies at the side and the monasteries praising, What a fine abbot. But when asked about the Buddha
Dharma, it is just like frying sand to make rice: nothing can be done with it, nothing can be put in the mouth; yet they say others
are wrong and I am right, their faces flushed red. This truly comes from worldly people uttering unlawful words. Those who truly wish to understand
this meaning, do not let this old monk down.

A monk asked, In the world, speaking Dharma for all sages all belongs to draped forms; how doesmaster instruct people?
The Master said, Where do you see this old monk? The student said, Please speak, master. The Master said, The whole hall of monks and teachers
does not understand this monk's words. Another monk asked, Please speak, master. The Master said, You speak,
and I will listen.

A monk asked, When true transformation leaves no trace and there is no teacher and disciple, how is it? The Master said, Who told you to come ask? The student
said, It was no one else. The Master then struck him.

A monk asked, How is this matter discerned? The Master said, I blame you. The student said, How can it be discerned? The Master said, I
blame you for not discerning. The student said, Should one preserve and verify it or not? The Master said, Preserve it or not, see for yourself.
A monk asked, What is a person without knowledge and understanding? The Master said, What matter are you talking about?

A monk asked, What is the meaning of coming from the West? The Master got down from the meditation seat. The student said, Is that it or not? The Master said, This old
monk has not yet spoken.

A monk asked, The Buddha-Dharma is long enduring; how should one use mind? The Master said, Did you see the Former Han and Later Han grasp and hold
the empire? At death they did not get even half a coin.

A monk asked, People of the world prize jewels; what does a śramaṇa prize? The Master said, Quickly shut your mouth.
The student said, If I shut my mouth, is that right or not? The Master said, If your mouth is not shut, how can you discern it?

A monk asked, What is Zhaozhou's one phrase? The Master said, Not even half a phrase. The student said, Is there not even master
here? The Master said, This old monk is not one phrase.

A monk asked, How can one avoid being deluded by circumstances? The Master dropped one foot down, and the monk immediately took off a shoe. The Master drew back
his foot, and the monk had no words.

A lay official asked, When the Buddha was in the world, all beings took refuge in Buddha. After the Buddha's nirvana, all
beings take refuge in what? The Master said, There are not yet any beings. The student said, I am asking right now. The Master said:
What Buddha are you still looking for?

A monk asked, Is there anyone who does not repay the four favors and the three realms or not? The Master said, Yes. The student said, Who is it?
The Master said, You father-killing fellow, count yourself as only lacking this one question.
A monk asked, What is master's meaning? The Master said, A place with no setting forth.

The Master ascended the hall and said: Brothers, just amend the past and cultivate the future. If you do not amend, there are plenty of places that will stick to you.
This old monk has been here more than thirty years, and not once has a Chan master come here. Even if
one does come, after one night and one meal he hurries off, chasing after some soft warm place.
A monk asked, If suddenly a Chan master comes, what should one say to him? The Master said, A thousand-caty crossbow is not cocked for
a mouse. The Master said, Brothers, if one comes from the south, then unload him; if
one comes from the north, then load him. Therefore it is said: If those near the top ask the Way, they lose the Way; if those near
the bottom ask the Way, they attain the Way. Brothers, when an upright person speaks deviant Dharma, the deviant Dharma also follows the upright;
when a deviant person speaks right Dharma, the right Dharma also follows the deviant. In other places, hard to see and easy to recognize; here with me, easy
to see and hard to recognize.

A monk asked, A person who cannot get hold of good and evil, can he be liberated alone or not? The Master said, Not liberated alone. The student said:
Why not liberated alone? The Master said, Right within good and evil.

A nun asked, Leaving aside what is said above, please instruct me, master. The Master barked, Smothered and broken iron bottle.
The nun brought an iron bottle filled with water and said, Please answer, master. The Master laughed at her.

A monk asked, If the world turns into a black pit, on what road does this one fall? The Master said, Not taking a share. The student
said, Who is the one not taking a share? The Master said, A field-granary slave.

A monk asked, Only when there is no speech and no intention does it count as a phrase. Since there is no speech, what is called a phrase? The Master said:
High yet not precarious, full yet not overflowing. The student said, Right now is master full or overflowing? The Master said:
What can I do about your asking me?

A monk asked, What is the numinous one? The Master said, On clean ground, a heap of shit. The student said, Please give the master's
true meaning. The Master said, Do not bother this old monk.

A monk asked, The Dharma-body is non-acting and does not fall into categories; is speech about it permitted or not? The Master said, How would you speak?
The student said, Then in that case it cannot be spoken. The Master laughed at him.

A monk asked, What is Buddha? What are sentient beings? The Master said, Sentient beings are Buddha, and Buddha is
sentient beings. The student said, Of the two, which one is the sentient being? The Master said, Ask, ask.

A monk asked, The great Way has no root; how does one respond in chant? The Master said, You just respond in chant. He said, If it has no root, then how
can it be? The Master said, Since it is rootless, where can it bind you?

A monk asked, Can one truly practicing avoid being measured by ghosts and spirits or not? The Master said, They can measure him. He said, The fault
lies where? The Master said, The fault lies in the seeking. He said, Then in that case there is no practice? The Master said:
Practice.
A monk asked, The lone moon hangs in the sky; from where does its light arise? The Master said, From where does the moon arise?

A monk asked, I have heard master say: The Way does not belong to practice, only do not be defiled. What is non-defilement?
The Master said, Examine inside and outside. He said, Does one examine oneself too or not? The Master said, Examine. He said, What fault does oneself
have, to examine oneself? The Master said, What business do you have?

The Master ascended the hall and said: This matter is like a bright jewel in the palm: when a Hu comes, a Hu appears; when a Han comes, a Han appears. This old
monk uses a blade of grass as a sixteen-foot golden body, and uses the sixteen-foot golden body as a blade of grass.
Buddha is affliction, affliction is Buddha. A monk asked, For whom does Buddha become affliction? The Master
said, For all people he becomes affliction. He said, How can one avoid it? The Master said, What use is avoidance?

The Master instructed the assembly, Here this old monk receives people with the fundamental matter. If you want this old monk to follow
their faculties and capacities in receiving them, the Three Vehicles and the Twelve Divisions of Teaching already receive them well enough. If they do not
understand, whose fault is it? Later, when you meet a true adept, you can also say this old monk did not wrong him. Whenever
anyone asks, receive him with the fundamental matter.

A monk asked, From ancient times until now, mind itself is Buddha. If not mind itself, is a student still permitted to discuss it or not?
The Master said, Set aside mind itself for the moment; what is there to discuss?
A monk asked, If an ancient mirror is not polished, does it still reflect or not? The Master said, Former life is cause, this life is result.

A monk asked, Before the three blades fall, how is it? The Master said, Dense and bristling. He said, After they fall, how is it? The Master said:
Far and open.
A monk asked, What is a person beyond the three realms? The Master said, Cannot be caged or covered.

A monk asked, Before Niutou met the Fourth Patriarch, a hundred birds brought flowers in offering; after meeting him, why did the hundred birds
no longer bring flowers in offering? The Master said, Responding to the world, not responding to the world.
A monk asked, What is it like when white clouds are at ease? The Master said, How could it compare to spring wind idle everywhere?

A monk asked, What is the white ox in the open field? The Master said, Under moonlight no color is needed. He said, What does it eat?
The Master said, From ancient to present none can chew it. He said, Please answer, Master. The Master said, This old monk answers like this.

The Master instructed the assembly: If mind intends, it at once errs. A monk then asked, What about when mind does not intend? The Master struck him twice
and said, Is it that this old monk has let you down, Ācārya?

A monk asked, Whenever there is question and answer, it falls into the faculty of mind; if it does not fall into the faculty of mind, how does the Master respond? The Master said, Ask.
The student said, Then please speak, Master. The Master said, Do not make right and wrong here.
A monk asked, When the dragon girl personally offered to the Buddha, what exactly did she offer? The Master made an offering gesture with both hands.

The Master instructed the assembly: Here the Buddha-Dharma, if called difficult is easy, if called easy is difficult. Elsewhere it is hard to see
and easy to know; here with this old monk it is easy to see and hard to know. If you can understand it, you may roam freely under heaven. Suppose
someone asks, From where do you come? If you say to him, From Zhaozhou, that slanders Zhaozhou; if
you say, Not from Zhaozhou, that buries yourself. Everyone, how will you answer him? A monk
asked, If whatever meets the eye slanders master, how can one avoid slandering? The Master said, If you say not slandering, already
that is slander.

A monk asked, What is the right path of practice? The Master said, If you understand practice, that is enough. If you do not understand practice,
you fall unevenly into others' cause and effect. He also said, I will teach you what to say. If someone asks, just say to him:
Zhaozhou comes. If he then asks, What Dharma does Zhaozhou teach? just say to him: When cold, speak of
cold; when hot, speak of heat. If he further asks, I am not asking about that matter, then simply say: What matter are you asking about?
If he asks again, What Dharma does Zhaozhou teach? then say to him: When master came, he did not tell me to pass on
messages. If you want to know Zhaozhou's matter, just go and ask him yourself.
A monk asked, What about when one does not look before or after? The Master said, Setting aside not looking before or after, whom are you asking?

The Master instructed the assembly: Kāśyapa transmitted to Ānanda. Tell me, to whom did Bodhidharma transmit?
A monk asked, Then when the Second Patriarch obtained the marrow, how was it? The Master said, Do not slander the Second Patriarch. The Master also said:
Bodhidharma also had words: Those outside get the skin, those inside get the bones. Tell me, those still further inside
what do they get?
A monk asked, What is the principle of obtaining the marrow? The Master said, Just recognize the skin. Here with this old monk, marrow
is not even established. He said, What is marrow then? The Master said, Like this, you have not even touched the skin.

A monk asked, So grand and imposing, is this not the master's proper position? The Master said, Do you know there is one who does not consent
to it? The student said, Then is there another position? The Master said, Who is the separate one? The student said, Who is the not-
separate one? The Master said, Shout as you please.

A monk asked, A top-top person turns with one nudge; what when a bottom-bottom person comes? The Master said, Are you top-
top or bottom-bottom? He said, Please answer, master. The Master said, The words have not yet found their master. He said, I have come seven
thousand li to come here; do not make it mind-activity. The Master said, Judging by this one question of yours, can mind-activity be avoided?
This monk stayed one night and then left.

A monk asked, What about one who does not continue what comes from the side? The Master said, Who? The student said, Huiyan. The Master said, Asked what?
The student said, One who does not continue what comes from the side. The Master stroked him with his hand.
A monk asked, What is the matter beneath the patched robe? The master said, Do not deceive yourself.

A monk asked, If suchness and ordinary and holy are all dream-talk, what is true speech? The master said, If I say more, then there are
two. The student said, Leave the two aside for now; what is true speech? The master said, Om lin.
A monk asked, What is Zhaozhou? The master said, East gate, west gate, south gate, north gate.

A monk asked, What is samadhi? The master said, Not fixed. The student said, Why not fixed? The master said, A living thing,
a living thing.

A monk asked, What about when one does not follow all forms of being? The master said, You should learn just so. He said, Is this not then the student’s
proper matter? The master said, Follow, follow.

A monk asked, The ancients, in thirty years, drew one bow and loosed two arrows, and only shot half a sage. Today
I ask the master to shoot the whole one. The master then got up and left.

The master addressed the assembly, The highest Way is not difficult; it only avoids picking and choosing. As soon as there are words, there is choosing. This old
monk is not in clarity. Where then do you see the Patriarch? A monk asked, Since you, master,
are not in clarity, what place are you protecting? The master said, I do not know either. The student said, master,
since you yourself do not know, why say you are not in clarity? The master said, You have asked the matter; now bow
and withdraw.

The master addressed the assembly, The Dharma is originally unborn, so now there is no extinction. Do not further say: speaking is birth,
not speaking is silence. All of you, how is the principle of nonbirth and nonextinction? A monk asked, Is grass
nonborn and nonextinct? The master said, This fellow only recognizes dead words.

A monk asked, The highest Way is not difficult; it only avoids picking and choosing. As soon as there are words, there is choosing. How then does the master
instruct people? The master said, Why not quote the ancients all the way through? The student said, I only said as far as
there. The master said, Just this: The highest Way is not difficult; it only avoids picking and choosing.

Ascending the hall, the master addressed the assembly, Reading sutras is in birth and death; not reading sutras is also in birth and death.
Everyone, how do you get out? A monk then asked, What if one leaves neither of the two behind?
The master said, If it is real, then yes; if not real, how could you get out of birth and death?

A monk asked, What is it when the sharpness is at the edge of a keen sword? The master said, This old monk is the keen sword; where is the sharpness
located?
A monk asked, When great danger arrives, how does one avoid it? The master said, Just right.

Ascending the hall, after a long pause, the master said, Has the whole assembly come yet? They replied, They have all come. The master said, Wait for one more
person to come, then I will speak. A monk said, When no one comes, then please tell us, master. The master said, It is very hard
to find such a person.

The master addressed the assembly, When mind arises, all dharmas arise; when mind ceases, all dharmas cease. You people,
what do you make of this? A monk then asked, What about when there is neither arising nor ceasing? The master said, I allow
you that one question.

During an interview, the master said, Bright yet not bright, the way is dim and about to dawn, at which end are you? The monk said:
Not at either end. The master said, Then you are in the middle? He said, If one is in the middle, then one is at
both ends. The master said, This monk has been with this old monk for quite some time, yet in speaking like this he still cannot
get out of the three phrases. And even if you did get out, you would still be within the three phrases. What do you do then? The monk
said, I can use the three phrases. The master said, Why did you not say so sooner?
A monk asked, What is the all-sided way? The master said, Leave aside Vajra Zen.

The master addressed the assembly, A patch-robed monk must sit clear through the heads of the reward-body and transformation-body Buddhas, only then will it do. A monk asked, The one who sits clear through
the heads of the reward-body and transformation-body Buddhas, what kind of person is that? The master said, Not your realm.

The master addressed the assembly, The great Way is right before your eyes, yet it is hard to see. A monk then asked, What form is there before us
for the student to behold? The master said, Whether you are south of the river or north of the river, as you please. The student said, master, do you not
have a skillful means for people? The master said, What did you just ask?

A monk asked, Upon entering the Dharma-realm, is there still knowing or not? The master said, Who enters the Dharma-realm? The student said, Then
having entered the Dharma-realm, is there no knowing? The master said, It is not cold ashes and dead wood; embroidered flowers appear
in hundreds of forms. The student said, Is there then functioning at the place of entering the Dharma-realm or not? The master said, What involvement is there?
tread."

A monk asked, If it is the ground of ultimate reality, from what place does it come? The master said, Venerable, please state it
once more.

A monk asked, When the myriad conditions all arise, is there one who cannot be deluded or not? The master said, There is. The student said, What is
the one who cannot be deluded? The master said, Do you still believe there is Buddha-Dharma? The student said, I believe there is Buddha-Dharma.
The ancients have spoken of it; what is the one who cannot be deluded? The master said, Why not ask this old monk?
The student said, I already asked. The master said, Deluded.

A monk asked, I wonder whether people of old and people of today are close to one another or not? The master said, Close, yes close,
but not one body. The student said, Why not the same? The master said, The dharma-body does not preach Dharma. The student said:
If the dharma-body does not preach Dharma, do you, master, teach people or not? The master said, I answer over in Hui. The student
said, Then how can you say the dharma-body does not preach Dharma? The master said, I rescue your father over in Hui, and he still
never shows his head.

A student asked, “If someone’s practice is ‘not meeting anyone,’ is there still any mutual turning, or not?” The teacher said, “If you can size it up, then there’s mutual turning.” The student
said, “If you can’t size it up, what mutual turning are you talking about?” The teacher said, “If you don’t put it that way, then it’s just you.” The student
“Then why do you hide yourself?” The teacher said, “I’m looking at you right now, talking with you.” The student said, “Then why say it doesn’t ‘turn’?” The teacher said, “That’s exactly how it should be."
Why do you hide yourself? The master said, Right now I am plainly talking with you. The student said, Then why
say it does not turn? The master said, It should be just so.Translation improved

The master addressed the assembly, To teach one who can be taught is a matter of this life; to teach one who cannot be taught is
an enemy in the third life. If one does not teach, one fears letting all beings fall. Teaching too is enmity.
Do you then teach or not? A monk said, I teach. The master said, Do all beings then see you
or not? The student said, They do not. The master said, Why do they not? The student said, No form. The master said, Right
now, do you see this old monk or not? The student said, master is not a sentient being. The master said, If you know your own fault,
that will do.

The master addressed the assembly, The dragon girl offers with intimate heart; it is entirely a natural matter. A monk asked, Since it is a natural offering,
why then? The master said, If she did not offer, how would one know it is natural?
The master addressed the assembly, Among eight hundred fellows who make Buddhas, to find one person of the Way is hard indeed.

A monk asked, In a place with no Buddha and no person, is there still cultivation or not? The master said, Aside from those two,
there are hundreds of thousands of millions. The student said, When the person of the Way comes, where are they? The master said, Then you
are not cultivating. The monk bowed. The master said, There is a great place to settle you.

A monk asked, What is it when the white clouds do not descend? The master said, This old monk does not understand the upper image. The student said, Is there really no
host and guest? The master said, This old monk is host, you are guest; where are the white clouds?
A monk asked, What is it when great skill seems clumsy? The master said, The timber fit for beams is lost.

The master addressed the assembly, The single word Buddha is one I do not like to hear. A monk asked, Do you still teach people, master?
The master said, I do. The student said, How do you teach people? The master said, Not knowing the mysterious meaning, one vainly thinks of stillness.
The student said, If it is mysterious, what is the meaning? The master said, I do not hold the root text. The student said, This
is the mysterious; what is the meaning? The master said, Answering you is the meaning.

The master addressed the assembly, Each has their own Chan, each has their own Way. If suddenly someone asks you, How
is Chan and how is the Way? how will you answer him? A monk then asked, Since each has Chan and Way, why from ancient times
down to now all this talk and speech? The master said, For your wandering soul. The student said, I wonder how one teaches
people? The master then withdrew and said nothing.

The master addressed the assembly, Do not pass your time idly; recite Buddha, recite Dharma. A monk then asked, What is the student’s own
recitation? The master said, Who is the one reciting? The student said, Without companion. The master shouted, You donkey.

Ascending the hall, the master addressed the assembly, If it is the first phrase, then take the patriarchs and Buddhas as teacher; with the second phrase, take humans and gods
as teacher; with the third phrase, self-rescue has no remedy. A monk asked, What is the first phrase? The master
said, Take the patriarchs and Buddhas as teacher. The master also said, Very good, start from the beginning. The student asked again, and the master said:
There go humans and gods again.

The master addressed the assembly, It is not that he does not bring it; it is not that this old monk does not respond. A monk said:
With what do you respond, master? The master gave a long sigh. The monk said, Using that as your response,
do you not fail the student? The master said, If just now you approved me, then I fail you; if
you do not approve me, then I do not fail you.

The master addressed the assembly, Tonight this old monk will answer questions; whoever can ask, come forward. As soon as a monk
came out and bowed, the master said, All along I have thrown a brick to draw jade, and got only a clod.

A monk asked, Does a dog have Buddha-nature or not? The master said, No. The student said, From all Buddhas above down to
ants below, all have Buddha-nature; why does a dog not? The master said, Because it has karmic consciousness-nature
present.

A monk asked, What is the dharma-body? The master said, The responsive body. He said, I am not asking about the responsive body. The master said, You
just attend to the responsive body.

A monk asked, What is it when the bright moon hangs in the sky? The master said, What is your name, venerable? The student said, Zhuanjia. The master
said, Where is the bright moon in the sky?

A monk asked, What is it right at sixteen? The master said, East east, west west. The student said, What is
east east, west west? The master said, It cannot be found.

A monk asked, What if the student does not understand at all? The master said, I understand even less. He said, Do you still know,
or not? The master said, I am not wood; how could I not know? He said, Excellent not understanding. The master
clapped his hands and laughed at him.
A monk asked, What is a person of the Way? The master said, I would say, a Buddha-person.

A monk asked, Whenever there are words and phrases, raising hands or moving feet, all of it falls into the student’s net. Apart from this, please
master, speak. The master said, This old monk has eaten the meal, but has not yet had tea.

Magistrate Ma asked, master, do you still cultivate practice or not? The master said, If this old monk cultivated practice, it would be a disaster
affair. He said, Since master does not cultivate, whom do you teach to cultivate? The master said, Magistrate, are you a
person who cultivates? He said, How am I called a cultivator? The master said, If you do not cultivate, how did you get thrown
into the kingly office among men? Fed into this red-frozen crimson ground, with no term for release? The magistrate then
shed tears, bowed, and gave thanks.

The master instructed the assembly, saying, "Venerable, it is not that you did not bring it; this old monk is not one who does not respond." He also said:
Venerable, do not raise your fist or join your palms; this old monk will not answer with the Chan seat or the whisk.

A monk asked, What is the place where thought and memory do not reach? The master said, Come over to this side. He said, Coming over to this side is
exactly the reachable place. What is the place thought does not reach? The master raised his hand and said, What do you call this?
He said, I call it a hand. What do you call it, master? The master said, I too can say a hundred names. He said:
I cannot match master in a hundred names, but what do you call it then? The master said, Then this is your place where thought and memory
do not reach. The monk bowed. The master said, I will teach you the place where thought and memory do reach. He said, What is it? The master
said, Shakyamuni’s teaching and the Patriarchs’ teaching are your teachers. He said, Patriarchs and Buddhas, the ancients already spoke of them. What then
is the place thought and memory do not reach? The master again raised his finger and said, What do you call this? The monk was silent for a long while. The master
said, Why not speak it right on the spot? What more is there to doubt?

A monk asked, What is your family style, master? The master said, This old monk is hard of hearing. Ask louder. The monk asked again,
and the master said, You ask about my family style, but I recognize yours.

A monk asked, What when the myriad conditions all arise? The master said, The myriad conditions all arise. He said, One question and one answer is
arising. What is non-arising? The master said, The Chan seat is the non-arising one. As the monk was just about to bow, the master
said, Do you remember the question and answer? He said, I remember. The master said, Try to state it. The monk was about to speak.

A monk asked, What is the Buddha before us? The master said, The one in the hall. He said, That is the form-Buddha; what
is Buddha? The master said, Mind itself is. He said, Mind itself is still limited. What is Buddha? The master
said, No-mind is. The student said, Do you allow the student to choose between mind and no-mind or not? The master said, Mind and
no-mind have both already been chosen by you. What more do you want this old monk to say?

A monk asked, I have come from far away to rely on the master; I wonder what your family style is? The master said, I do not speak it to people. The student said, Why
do you not speak it to people? The master said, That is my family style. The student said, Since master does not speak it to
people, what about those who come from the four seas to rely on you? The master said, Are you saying I am not a sea? The student said, I wonder
what the matters within the sea are like? The master said, This old monk has hooked one.

A monk asked, What kind of person is it that patriarchs and Buddhas cannot approach? The master said, Not patriarchs, not Buddhas. The student said, But
what about their not being able to approach? The master said, I tell you: not patriarchs, not Buddhas, not sentient beings, not
things, is that enough? The student said, Then what is it? The master said, If it has a name, then it is patriarchs, Buddhas, sentient beings,
and so on. The student said, It cannot just be left at that, can it? The master said, I have not yet let you go.
A monk asked, What is the ordinary mind? The master said, Foxes, wolves, and wild jackals.

A monk asked, By what expedient can one hear what has never been heard? The master said, Leave what has never been heard aside for now; what have you ever heard
in the first place?

A monk asked, I have heard the teaching speak of the wish-fulfilling jewel that follows forms; what is the original color? The master called the monk by name, and the monk
The monk answered. The master said, Come over to this side. The monk then came over and asked again, What is original color? The master said:
For now, just follow the colors along.

A monk asked, Does a person of ordinary mind still receive instruction or not? The master said, I do not pass through another's gate.
The student said, If so, then would that not sink the person over there? The master said, Excellent ordinary mind.
A monk asked, What is the thing to be safeguarded in practice? The master said, Through the end of future ages, it cannot be picked out.
A monk asked, What is a person of great cultivation? The master said, The temple officers are.

A monk asked, When a student has just arrived and knows nothing at all of the gate and threshold, what then? The master said, What is your name, Reverend?
The student said, Huinan. The master said, Fine indeed, not knowing.

A monk asked, This student wants to learn, yet he slanders the master; how can he avoid slandering? The master said, What is your name?
The student said, Daojiao. The master said, Go sit quietly, you rice bin.

A monk asked, What is the master's great meaning? The master said, No great, no small. The student said, Is that then the master's
great meaning? The master said, If there is the slightest bit, then for ten thousand kalpas it will not do.
A monk asked, All dharmas are originally at rest, but people themselves make trouble. Whose words are these? The master said, Step out and you die.

A monk asked, Not Buddha, not thing, not sentient being, this is a cutting statement. What is an uncutting
statement? The master said, Above heaven and below heaven, I alone am honored.

A monk asked, What is Vairocana's perfect circle? The master said, This old monk left home from youth and has never had failing eyes.
The student said, Does the master still teach people or not? The master said, May you always see Vairocana's perfect circle.

A monk asked, While Buddhas and patriarchs were present, they transmitted to one another. After Buddhas and patriarchs are gone, who transmits? The master said, From ancient times
until now it all falls to this old monk. The student said, I do not know what is transmitted. The master said, Every one of them all
belongs to birth and death. The monk said, You must not bury the patriarchs. The master said, Transmit what?

A monk asked, What is it like when both ordinary and holy are exhausted? The master said, May you become a great worthy. This old monk is one who obstructs Buddhas
and patriarchs.
A monk asked, I heard of Zhaozhou from afar, but after arriving why do I not see him? The master said, It is this old monk's fault.

A monk asked, The bright moon is in the sky. I do not know how things are in the room. The master said, Since leaving home, this old monk has never
made a livelihood. The student said, Then the master does not act for the present age? The master said, One's own illness
cannot be cured, how could one cure all illnesses? The student said, What is the student to do with no support? The master said, If you rely,
you step on the ground. If you do not rely, then east or west as you please.

A monk asked, What is it like when mind is in mind and mind cannot measure it? The master said, Measure whom? The student said, Measure myself. The master
said, There are not two.

A monk asked, What is it like when no boundary or mark is seen? The master pointed to the clean bottle and said, What is this? The student said, A clean bottle.
The master said, Excellent not seeing boundary marks.
A monk asked, What is returning to the root? The master said, The moment you intend it, you miss.

A monk asked, Without leaving words and phrases, how can one attain solitary liberation? The master said, Leaving words and phrases is solitary liberation. The student said:
Just now no one told me to come here. The master said, Then what brought you here? The student said, Why does the master
not pick it out? The master said, I picked it out long ago.
A monk asked, Not mind and not identical with wisdom, please give one phrase, master. The master said, This old monk falls behind you.

A monk asked, What is the ultimate? The master said, The ultimate. The student said, Which one is the ultimate? The master said, This old
monk is the ultimate; you do not understand how to ask this question. The student said, It is not that I do not ask. The master said, The ultimate
is in what place?

A monk asked, What is it like when not hanging even an inch of thread? The master said, Not hanging what? The student said, Not hanging an inch of thread.
The master said, Excellent not hanging an inch of thread.

A monk asked, What about a person like one saving his head from fire? The master said, Just study. The student said, Where? The master said:
Do not occupy another's rank.

A monk asked, In the empty kalpa, who is the master? The master said, This old monk sits right there. The student said, What
dharma do you preach? The master said, I preach what you asked.

A monk asked, It is said of old, empty clarity illumines itself. What is self-illumination? The master said, It is not called another's illumination.
The student said, What about where illumination does not reach? The master said, Your words have fallen.
A monk asked, What is the target, the exact point? The master said, When not a single thought has arisen.

A monk asked, What is the Dharma King? The master said, The great king of the province is. The monk said, Is the master not? The master said:
Are you planning rebellion? Altogether you do not recognize even one king.

A monk asked, What is Buddha-mind? The master said, You are mind, I am Buddha; whether you serve or not, see for yourself. The student
said, The master is certainly there, but can one still serve or not? The master said, You teach me and let me see.
A monk asked, Among the three bodies, which is the original body? The master said, Not one may be lacking.

A monk asked, I do not know who is patriarch in this land. The master said, Since Bodhidharma came here, all are so. The student said:
Which patriarchal generation is the master? The master said, I do not fall into rank. The student said, Where is it? The master
said, In your ears.

A monk asked, Not abandoning the root and not chasing branches, what is the right path? The master said, Fine monk, child of leaving home. The student
said, This student has never left home. The master said, Take refuge in Buddha, take refuge in Dharma. The student said, I do not know whether
there is even a home to leave or not. The master said, You must directly leave home. The student said, Where should one place
it? The master said, For now, sit at home.

A monk asked, A clear-eyed person sees everything, but does he still see form or not? The master said, Strike it away. The student said, How
can one strike it? The master said, Do not use force. The student said, If one does not use force, how can one strike it? The master said, If you use
force, you go astray.

A monk asked, The great meaning of patriarchs and Buddhas, for what kind of person is it? The master said, Only for people of the present time. The student said, What is to be done
if one cannot manage it? The master said, Whose fault is that? The student said, How should one undertake it? The master said, Nowadays no one
can undertake it. The monk said, Then there is no support to rely on? The master said, Yet you cannot do without this old monk.

A monk asked, What about one who has finished the matter? The master said, Precisely great cultivation. The student said, I do not know whether the master still
cultivates or not. The master said, Putting on clothes and eating food. The student said, Putting on clothes and eating food are ordinary things, I do not know whether that is
cultivation or not. The master said, You tell me first, what do I do every day?

Vice Minister Cui asked, Does a great good spiritual friend also enter hell or not? The master said, This old monk is the last to
enter. Cui said, If he is a great good spiritual friend, why enter hell? The master said, If this old monk
did not enter, how could I get to see you, Vice Minister?

A monk asked, What if there is a difference of a hair's breadth? The master said, Heaven and earth are far apart. The monk said, What if there is no difference of a hair's breadth?
The master said, Heaven and earth are far apart.

A monk asked, What are eyes that do not sleep? The master said, Ordinary eyes, fleshly eyes. He also said, Though one has not yet attained heavenly
eyes, the power of the fleshly eyes is like this. The student said, What are sleeping eyes? The master said, Buddha-eye and Dharma-eye
are sleeping eyes.

A monk asked, At Dayu Ridge one caught up in time, so why could it not be lifted? The master picked up his robe and said:
Where did you get this from? The monk said, I am not asking about this. The master said, Then in that case it cannot be lifted.

A monk asked, If not joined and not dispersed, how does one distinguish? The master said, You have one, I have one. The monk said, This
one is joining, what is dispersing? The master said, You are just joining.
A monk asked, What is the road that does not miss? The master said, Knowing mind and seeing nature is the road that does not miss.
A monk asked, If a bright pearl is in the palm, does it shine or not? The master said, Shining is certainly not absent, but what do you call the pearl?

A monk asked, What is it like when the numinous sprout has no root? The master said, Where have you come from? The monk said, From Taiyuan.
The master said, Excellent rootless.

A monk asked, What if a student intends to become a Buddha? The master said, That is terribly strenuous. The monk said, If there is no effort,
then what? The master said, Then go ahead and become Buddha.

A monk asked, This student is dull and sunk in rising and falling; how can he get out? The master just sat there. The monk said, I truly
am asking the master. The master said, Where are you making one rise and one sink?

A monk asked, Neither in the ordinary nor in the holy, how can one avoid the road of two ends? The master said, Remove the two ends
and come answer me. The monk did not understand. The master said, Not understanding, from where does it arise? Here, it arises from this old
monk. When you are in the marketplace, from where does it arise? The monk said, Why is the master not fixed? The master
said, I teach you, why not say, Today the wind is fine.

A monk asked, What is a great icchantika person? The master said, If this old monk answers you, will you believe it? The monk said, The
master's word is weighty, how would I dare not believe? The master said, It is hard to find an icchantika person.

A monk asked, A greatly shameless person, where can he be placed? The master said, He cannot be placed here. The monk said:
If he suddenly sticks his head out, what then? The master said, Take him away.
A monk asked, What is it like when function does not appear? The master said, Function is certainly not absent, but who is appearance?

A monk asked, In the empty kalpa, is there still anyone cultivating or not? The master said, What do you call an empty kalpa? The monk said:
Not a single thing is. The master said, Only this is called cultivation. What do you call an empty kalpa?
A monk asked, What is leaving home? The master said, Do not tread on high reputation; do not seek foul ruin.

A monk asked, Without pointing to a single dharma, what is the master's dharma? The master said, This old monk does not speak Maoshan dharma.
The monk said, Since you do not speak Maoshan dharma, what is the master's dharma? The master said, I told you I do not speak
Maoshan dharma. The monk said, Is this then it or not? The master said, This old monk has never taken this
and shown it to people.

A monk asked, What is the single path of liberation right before one's eyes? The master said, No two and also no three. The monk said, Right before my eyes
there is a path; is the student permitted to advance or not? The master said, If so, then a thousand miles, ten thousand miles.

A monk asked, What is the matter beyond Vairocana? The master said, This old monk is under your feet. The monk said, master
why are you under the student's feet? The master said, You originally do not know there is a matter beyond.

A monk asked, What is fitting the head? The master said, It is you not fitting the head. The monk said, What is not fitting the head?
The master said, Pick it out in the previous phrase.
A monk asked, What is the master's exact meaning? The master said, Stop, stop, no need to speak. My Dharma is subtle and hard to conceive.

A monk asked, What is it like when clear and limpid, without a speck? The master said, Falling into a pit, dropping into a ditch. The monk said, What fault is there? The master
said, Why do you wrong such a person?

A monk asked, I do not know, when one leaves home and vows to seek unsurpassed bodhi, what then? The master said, Before leaving home, one is driven by
bodhi; after leaving home, one can make bodhi serve.

A scholar, seeing the master's staff in his hand, said, Buddha does not take away sentient beings' wishes, is that so? The master
said, Yes. The scholar said, Then may I ask the master for the staff in your hand? The master said:
A gentleman does not take what another favors. The scholar said, I am no gentleman. The master said, This old monk
is no Buddha either.

The master was out and saw an old woman planting rice. He said, If you suddenly met a fierce tiger, what would you do? The old woman said, No
single dharma can match the mind. The master said, Drop it. The old woman said, Tu. The master said, Hard to have one like this around.

A scholar took leave, saying, I have troubled the master here for a long time and have nothing with which to repay
the master. Another day I will come back as a donkey to repay the master. The master said, Then how is this old monk to
get a saddle?

The master went to Daowu's place. As soon as he entered the monks' hall, Daowu said, A single arrow from Nanquan has come. The master said, Watch the
arrow. Daowu said, It has passed. The master said, It hit.

cb:juan.cb:jhead: Recorded Sayings of Master Zhaozhou, Upper ScrollEnd.
Recorded Sayings of Master Zhaozhou, Middle Scroll

cb:div.p:
The master ascended the hall and addressed the assembly, A golden Buddha does not pass through the furnace, a wooden Buddha does not pass through fire, a clay Buddha does not pass through water,
the true Buddha sits within. Bodhi, nirvana, suchness, Buddha-nature, all are clothes clinging to the body, and also
are called afflictions. If one does not ask, there are no afflictions. In the ground of ultimate reality, where can anything be set? If one mind does not
arise, the ten thousand dharmas are without fault. Just investigate the principle and sit for twenty or thirty years. If you do not understand, cut off this old
monk's head and take it. Dreams, illusions, empty flowers are vainly grasped. If mind is not different, the ten thousand dharmas are also thus. Since
it is not obtained from outside, what further binds you? Like a sheep, why blindly pick up things and stuff them in your mouth?
What are you doing? This old monk once heard Master Yaoshan say, If someone asks, just tell him to shut the dog's mouth. This old
monk also says, Shut the dog's mouth. Taking me is filth; not taking me is purity. It is just like a hunting dog
that only wants to get something to eat. Where can Buddhadharma be placed? A thousand people, ten thousand people, all
are fellows seeking Buddha; not one seeker of the Way can be found. If you would be a disciple of the Empty King, do not let
the sickness of mind, the hardest to cure, remain. Before there was a world, this nature was already there. When the world perishes, this nature does not
perish. From the moment you have seen this old monk once, you are no longer someone else, but simply the host himself. This one
why seek outside again? At such a time, do not turn your head and change your face, or you will lose it.

A monk asked, When the hundred bones all crumble and scatter, yet one thing remains ever numinous, what then? The master said, This morning again the wind
has risen.

A monk asked, I will not ask about the Three Vehicles and the twelve divisions of teaching. What is the Patriarch's meaning in coming from the West? The master said:
The water buffalo has calved, go and look well. The monk said, I do not understand this meaning. The master said, I also
do not know.
A monk asked, What is it like when the myriad nations come to court? The master said, Meeting people, you cannot call out.

A monk asked, Throughout the twelve hours, how does one wash out and sift? The master said, The Naih River is muddy, the western stream runs swift. The monk said:
Can one still see Manjusri or not? The master said, You muddle-eyed fellow, where have you been coming and going?

Asked, What is the place of practice? The master said, You come from the place of practice, you go to the place of practice; in your whole body
it is the place of practice. What place is not?

Asked, What is it like before the sprout has emerged? The master said, Smell it and your skull splits. He said, What about when one does not smell it,
then what? The master said, There is no such idle time.

Asked, What is counting and measure? The master said, One, two, three, four, five. He said, What about the matter not bound by counting and measure,
then what? The master said, One, two, three, four, five.

Asked, In what world is there no day and night? The master said, Right now, is it day or night? He said, I am not asking
about right now. The master said, What can you do about this old monk?

Asked, Kashyapa's upper robe, not treading the Caoxi road, who gets to wear it? The master said, Empty space
does not appear in the world; practitioners all do not know.

Asked, What is mingled yet not mixed? The master said, This old monk eats vegetables and keeps the long fast. He said, Can one still attain tran-
scendence or not? The master said, Break the fast.
Asked, What are the words of the ancients? The master said, Listen carefully, listen carefully.
Asked, What is the student's proper business? The master said, If it is thus, what are you dissatisfied with?

Asked, The ten thousand dharmas return to one; where does the one return? The master said, In Qingzhou I made a cloth robe,
it weighed seven jin.
Asked, What is a home-leaver? The master said, He does not bow to the Son of Heaven, and his parents bow back to him.
Asked, What is the matter face to face? The master said, You are a face to face fellow.
Asked, Who is the person beyond Buddha? The master said, The one right there leading the plow ox is.

Asked, What is urgency? The master said, This old monk speaks like this; what do you make of it? He said, I do not understand. The master
said, I tell you: in haste, put on your boots and stand on water; ride a horse to Chang'an, and the tips of your boots are still not
wet.
Asked, When the four mountains close in, what then? The master said, No road that is Zhaozhou.

Asked, When the ancient hall has no king, what then? The master coughed once. He said, Then is this the minister addressing the throne
below? The master said, The thief's body is already exposed.
Asked, How old are you, Master? The master said, A string of prayer beads cannot count it out.
Asked, Whose Dharma heir are you, Master? The master said, Congshen.

Asked, If someone elsewhere suddenly asks, What Dharma does Zhaozhou teach? how should one reply? The master said, Salt
is expensive, rice is cheap.
Asked, What is Buddha? The master said, Are you Buddha?
Asked, What is leaving home? The master said, How could you get to see this old monk?
Asked, What about where buddhas and patriarchs are unbroken? The master said, No leakage.

Asked, Original source, please instruct me. The master said, The original source has no illness. He said, What about the place of realization? The master said,
The realized person knows. He said, At such a time, then what? The master said, Give it a name for me.
Asked, When it is pure one without mixture, what then? The master said, A very fine question indeed.

Asked, Does a person of nonaction and stillness fall into dead emptiness or not? The master said, Falls into dead emptiness.
He said, Ultimately, how is it? The master said, Become a donkey, become a horse.

Asked, What is the Patriarch's meaning in coming from the West? The master said, The bed leg is it. He said, Is that it or not?
The master said, If it is, then take it off and carry it away.
Asked, When it is clear and spotless, what then? The master said, Here, this old monk does not employ hired laborers.
Asked, When even a phoenix cannot fly there, what then? The master said, From where did it arise?
Asked, In the ground of ultimate reality, when not a single mote is accepted, what then? The master said, Everything is altogether in there.
Asked, What is the one phrase? The master answered, Yes. The monk asked again. The master said, I do not suffer from deafness.
Asked, Does a newborn child already possess the six consciousnesses or not? The master said, Hitting a ball on swift flowing water.
Asked, When everything arrives, what then? The master said, It is still a hundred paces short of this old monk.

Asked, What is the master's family style? The master said, This old monk left home from childhood, and shook off the ragged means of living
completely.
Asked, Please speak apart from the four propositions. The master said, This old monk is always right in there.

Asked, Why does Bianque, the king of physicians, have illness? The master said, Bianque, king of physicians, does not leave the bed and pillow.
He also said, One drop of sweet dew moistens the great thousand worlds universally.
Asked, What is the white ox in the open ground? The master said, That beast.

Asked, What is the mark of a great person? The master said, Look at it sideways. He said, That is still approaching from below the steps,
is it not? The master said, This old monk has no time to go chasing that idle fellow.

Asked, The moment a thought arises, one falls among humans and gods; if there is utterly no thought, one falls among kin and attendants what then?
The master said, Not only this old monk, even an adept could not answer you.

Asked, Whenever there is action, it all falls into dregs; please answer without acting. The master scolded the nun, saying, Bring
water here and add it to the boiling cauldron.
Asked, What is prajna paramita? The master said, Maha prajna paramita.

Asked, What is the lion that bites people? The master said, I take refuge in Buddha, I take refuge in Dharma, I take refuge in Sangha do not
bite this old monk.
Asked, Leaving aside words and phrases, please speak, Master. The master coughed.
Asked, How can one avoid slandering the ancients and not betray their kindness? The master said, How do you do it, Reverend?
Asked, What is the one phrase? The master said, What are you saying?
Asked, What is the one phrase? The master said, Two phrases.
Asked, What about saying only the Buddha alone is a good spiritual friend? The master said, Demon talk.
Asked, What is bodhi? The master said, This one is icchantika.
Asked, What is the mark of a great person? The master said, A fine descendant indeed.
Asked, When it is silent and without support, what then? The master said, This old monk is behind your back.

Asked, What is the monastery? The master said, What else is there? He said, Who is the person in the monastery,
then? The master said, This old monk and you, Reverend.
Asked, When two dragons fight over a jewel, who gets it? The master said, This old monk just watches.

Asked, Who is the person beyond cause and effect? The master said, If not for your asking, Reverend, this old monk truly would not
know.

Asked, The many blind men feel the elephant and each speaks differently how is the true elephant? The master said, Without falsity, it is just
not known.

Asked, What is the first phrase? The master coughed. He said, Is that it or not? The master said, This old monk coughs
and may not?

Asked, Does the great ocean receive all streams or not? The master said, The great ocean says it does not know. He said, Why
does it not know? The master said, It never says, I receive all streams.
Asked, What is Vairocana? The master said, Vairocana, Vairocana.

Asked, Do the buddhas have a teacher or not? The master said, They do. He said, Who is the teacher of the buddhas? The master said,
Amitabha Buddha, Amitabha Buddha.

Asked, Who is the student's teacher? The master said, Clouds have the force of leaving the mountain; water has no sound of plunging into the ravine. He said,
I am not asking about that. The master said, It is just that you do not recognize your teacher.

Asked, Everywhere they all speak from the mouth; how do you show people, Master? The master struck the brazier with his heel
to show him. He said, Is that it or not? The master said, You have just recognized this old monk's heel.

Asked, When one does not walk the great way, what then? The master said, A fellow who peddles contraband salt. He said, And when one does walk the great way,
what then? The master said, Bring me your official permit.

Asked, What is the original body? The master said, Ever since he recognized this old monk, this fellow
has not been different. He said, Then am I separated from you by a lifetime, Master? The master said, Not only this life; for a thou-
sand lives, ten thousand lives, you still would not know this old monk.

Asked, What is the Patriarch's meaning in coming from the West? The master said, How long has the gourd been hanging on the east wall
there?

Asked, When square and round do not fit, what then? The master said, Not square, not round. He said, At such a time, then what?
The master said, It is square, it is round.
Asked, When practitioners meet, what then? The master said, Present lacquerware.

Asked, Why can truth not be contemplated? The master said, Truth is not nonexistent, but contemplation cannot get it. He said, In the end
what is it? The master said, Lost the truth.

Asked, When going does not reach and asking does not reach, what then? The master said, Reaching and not reaching a practitioner
sees as snot and spit. He said, What is the matter within that? The master spat on the ground.

Asked, What is the Patriarch's meaning in coming from the West? The master said, If you do not call it the Patriarch's meaning, still
it is not yet there. He said, What about the original? The master said, Four eyes facing each other; there is no second master.
Asked, Without possessing form and bearing, can one still understand or not? The master said, Right now, do you understand or not?
Asked, What is a person of great shamelessness? The master said, All are endowed with the inconceivable.

Asked, I intend to go south and study a bit of Buddhadharma how about it? The master said, When you go south,
if you see a place with Buddha, hurry past it; where there is no Buddha, do not stay. He said, Then the student
has no support? The master said, Willow fluff, willow fluff.
Asked, What is the urgent point? The master said, One question, one answer.

Asked, Without relying on three inches, does it still depend on the present time or not? The master said, I speak following you; how do you
understand it?

Asked, What is the master's family style? The master said, In the vast universe, people are without number. He said, I ask the mas-
ter not to answer with words. The master said, Should this old monk be like that?
Asked, When two dragons fight over a jewel, who gets it? The master said, The loser loses nothing; the winner has no use for it.
Asked, What is the mark of a great person? The master said, What is it?

A layman offered a kasaya and asked, Wearing such a robe, would one not betray the ancients? The master
threw down his whisk and said, Is this ancient or is this present?
Asked, What is the conduct of a shramana? The master said, Extend the hands, not the feet.

Asked, Before Niutou met the Fourth Patriarch, what was it like? The master said, Full of firewood, full of water. He said, After meeting him,
what then? The master said, Full of firewood, full of water.

Asked, What is the student's own self? The master said, Have you eaten your gruel yet? He said, I have eaten gruel. The master
said, Go wash your bowl.

Asked, What is Vairocana? The master said, Has the white camel come yet? He said, It has come. The master said, Lead it
away and feed it grass.
Asked, What is teacherless wisdom? The master said, This old monk has never taught you, Reverend.
Asked, What is the intimate phrase? The master said, The words have fallen.

Asked, Without borrowing the mouth, is discussion still permitted or not? The master said, Exactly at this time. He said, Then please discuss it, Master.
The master said, This old monk has never come out.

Asked, For what purpose did the Second Patriarch cut off his arm? The master said, Powdering bones and shattering the body. He said, To make offering to what
person? The master said, An offering to the one who comes.

Asked, Why does the Bodhisattva of boundless body not see the Tathagata's crown mark? The master said, You are the rev-
erend.
Asked, By day there is sunlight, by night there is firelight what is divine light? The master said, Sunlight, firelight.

Asked, What is the right place to ask? The master said, Wrong. He said, What is the place of not asking? The master said, In the previous
phrase, distinguish it.
Asked, What is the mark of a great person? The master touched his face with his hand, folded his hands, and composed his expression.
Asked, What is nonaction? The master said, This is action.
Asked, What is the Patriarch's meaning in coming from the West? The master said, The ox got lost from the pen.
Asked, I have come from afar; please instruct me, Master. The master said, The moment you enter the gate, it would be fitting to spit in your face.

Asked, What is the straight and direct road? The master said, Has the boatman from Huainan arrived yet? He said, This student
does not understand. The master said, For now, be glad you have arrived.

Asked, Does the cypress tree have buddha nature or not? The master said, It does. He said, When will it become Buddha? The master said,
When empty space falls to the ground. He said, When will empty space fall to the ground? The master said, When the cypress tree becomes Buddha.

Asked, What is the meaning of coming from the West? The master said, Why did you curse this old monk in the courtyard? He said, This stu-
dent has done no wrong. The master said, This old monk cannot go curse you in the courtyard.
Asked, What is the meaning of coming from the West? The master said, Hair grows on wooden teeth.

Asked, When a poor son comes, what do you give him? The master said, Not poor. He said, What can be done, if he seeks out the Master?
The master said, Just keep to poverty.

Asked, Why does the Bodhisattva of boundless body not see the Tathagata's crown mark? The master said, As if through gauze
silk.
Asked, Who gets to drink the sweet dew of the devas? The master said, Thank you for bringing it.
A monk asked, "What about one who has gone beyond heaven and earth?" The master said, "When there is such a person, report it to me."
Asked, "What is the monastery?" The master said, "The three gates and the Buddha hall."
Asked, "What is the unborn and undying?" The master said, "Originally it is unborn; now too it has no extinction."
Asked, "Who is the lord of Zhaozhou?" The master said, "The great king is."

Asked, "At a critical moment, please speak, Master." The master said, "Pissing is a small matter; it must be that this old monk himself goes, only then"
can it be done."

Asked, "What is the sixteen-foot golden body?" The master said, "Tuck the collar under the armpit." The monk said, "I do not understand." The master
said, "If you do not understand, ask someone to cut it."

Asked, "What when a student has doubts?" The master said, "Big convenience, small convenience." The student said, "A big doubt." The master said,
"For a big convenience, the northeast corner; for a small convenience, behind the monks' hall."

Asked, "Who is the person above Buddha?" The master got down from the Chan couch, looked him up and down, and said, "This fellow
is so tall and big; cut him into three stakes and it would do. Why ask about above and below?"

A nun asked, "What is the most secret meaning?" The master pinched her with his hand. She said, "master still has this thing!"
The master said, "It is you who have this thing."

The master addressed the assembly, saying, "Thirty years ago, in the south, at the side of the brazier, this old monk had a saying about no host and
no guest. Right up to now, no one has brought it up."

Asked, "The king supports you so generously, Master; with what do you repay him?" The master said, "Reciting Buddha." He said,
"Even a poor fellow can recite Buddha." The master said, "Call the attendant and give him one coin."
Asked, "What is the master's family style?" The master said, "Though the screen is broken, its frame still remains."

Asked, "What is the meaning of non-moving?" The master said, "Tell me, that wild duck flying from east to
west, did it move or not?"
Asked, "What is the meaning of coming from the West?" The master said, "Where did you get this news from?"
Asked, "What is a person in the dust?" The master said, "Give some tea-and-salt money."
